machine.name: Child Development Monitor
machine.description: Tracks developmental milestone progress, social-emotional engagement, language acquisition, and learning readiness for children ages 0–12 in low- and medium-income home care settings. Early identification of developmental delays in economically vulnerable households is critical because access to early intervention services (EI, speech therapy, occupational therapy) is income- and geography-dependent. Output at [2007:2011] feeds the Home Caregiver Burden Monitor alongside the Pediatric Nutrition Monitor.

metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.populationFocus: Children ages 0–12; families in early intervention program gaps; low-income households with limited developmental support access

sensorNormalization.milestone_completion_norm: 0.0=significant delay (>2 standard deviations behind age norms), 0.5=mild delay or age-appropriate borderline, 1.0=meeting or exceeding age milestones
sensorNormalization.peer_interaction_norm: 0.0=social withdrawal or isolation; no peer engagement, 0.5=limited but functional peer interaction, 1.0=age-appropriate social engagement
sensorNormalization.language_score_norm: 0.0=non-verbal or severely limited expressive/receptive language for age, 0.5=borderline language development, 1.0=age-appropriate or advanced language
sensorNormalization.learning_engagement_norm: 0.0=disengaged; unable to focus; school-readiness concerns, 0.5=partial engagement with support needs, 1.0=curious and engaged learner

governance.notes: Auto-backfilled by scripts/backfill-governance.mjs from primaryDomain="health-personal".  Review and tune the routing as the team owning ChildDevelopmentMonitor matures.

rules.description: REFERRAL_NEEDED: multi-domain developmental delay requiring early intervention referral at [2007:2011]
SOCIAL_DEVELOPMENTAL_CONCERN: child social-emotional withdrawal requiring behavioral health screening at [2007:2011]
DEVELOPMENT_OK: child development on track for age and context at [2007:2011]

sequences.name: Developmental Delay: TYPICAL+ → BORDERLINE → DELAYED → SIGNIFICANT_DELAY → REFERRAL_NEEDED

metadata.description: Tracks progressive developmental deviation from age norms. In low-income settings, developmental delays frequently go undetected until school age due to lack of well-child visit access. This pattern catches multi-domain developmental regression before the critical early intervention window closes at age 3–5.

metadata.name: TYPICAL
metadata.description: TYPICAL+ — milestones met; peer interaction present; language developing; learning engagement observed. isInitial A*A = A+.

metadata.name: BORDERLINE
metadata.description: BORDERLINE — one or more milestone domains lagging; language slightly behind; reduced learning engagement.
metadata.milestone_completion: 0.30–0.48 (1 standard deviation behind; watchful waiting zone)

metadata.name: DELAYED
metadata.description: DELAYED — multiple milestone domains behind; limited language; reduced engagement across settings.
metadata.milestone_completion: 0.15–0.28 (1.5–2 standard deviations behind; EI referral zone)

metadata.name: SIGNIFICANT_DELAY
metadata.description: SIGNIFICANT_DELAY — global developmental delay across milestones, language, and social-emotional domains. Fires REFERRAL_NEEDED for early intervention or specialist evaluation.
REFERRAL_NEEDED: multi-domain developmental delay requiring early intervention referral at [2007:2011]
metadata.action: Early intervention referral; developmental pediatrician evaluation; speech/OT/PT screening

sequences.name: Social Withdrawal: ENGAGED+ → WITHDRAWING → ISOLATED → SOCIAL_DEVELOPMENTAL_CONCERN

metadata.description: Tracks social-emotional withdrawal trajectory. In low-income households social isolation in children is driven by housing instability, caregiver stress, and limited play access. This pattern fires before full withdrawal and is associated with adverse childhood experience (ACE) accumulation.

metadata.name: ENGAGED
metadata.description: ENGAGED+ — child socially active with peers; language used for communication; responsive and curious. isInitial A*A = A+.

metadata.name: WITHDRAWING
metadata.description: WITHDRAWING — reduced peer initiation; quieter; less responsive; possible regression in established skills.

metadata.name: ISOLATED
metadata.description: ISOLATED — no peer interaction; avoidant behavior; minimal communication. Fires SOCIAL_DEVELOPMENTAL_CONCERN.
SOCIAL_DEVELOPMENTAL_CONCERN: child social-emotional withdrawal requiring behavioral health screening at [2007:2011]
metadata.action: Developmental-behavioral pediatrics referral; ACE screening; caregiver mental health check

sequences.name: Development On Track: ON_TRACK+ → DEVELOPMENT_OK signal

metadata.description: Fires DEVELOPMENT_OK when all developmental dimensions are within age-appropriate ranges. In low-income home health contexts, this confirms that existing caregiving and community support (Head Start, home visiting programs) are maintaining positive developmental trajectory.

metadata.name: ON_TRACK
metadata.description: ON_TRACK+ — all developmental dimensions above threshold; child thriving within available resources. Fires DEVELOPMENT_OK each matching cycle.
metadata.scenario: Child in Head Start or home visiting program; caregiver engaged; developmental milestones met

metadata.description: DEVELOPMENT_OK: child development on track for age and context at [2007:2011]
metadata.action: Caregiver burden monitor registers healthy child developmental state

inputSequences.name: Language Delay After Housing Disruption
inputSequences.description: Family experiences eviction; child's language and social development regress during shelter stay; early intervention referral and stabilization restore on-track trajectory.
